47,000
new HPV-related cancer diagnoses every year in the United States
36,000
caused directly by HPV
93%
preventable through vaccination
~59%
U.S. adolescent HPV vaccination rate
Most are preventable
HPV causes nearly all cervical cancers, most anal cancers, and a growing share of oropharyngeal cancers. Yet awareness remains dangerously low. Vaccination and early screening can prevent the vast majority of these diagnoses.
"No one should face an HPV-related cancer diagnosis alone. That conviction is what started this movement."
Lillian Kreppel, Co-Founder & CEO
The movement to eliminate HPV cancers
2024
HPVCA is founded
Actress Marcia Cross and activist Lillian Kreppel — both anal cancer survivors — launch the HPV Cancers Alliance.
2025
A national platform takes shape
HPVCA partners with AdventHealth for International HPV Awareness Day, convenes three expert panels, and launches the HPV Next Steps prevention tool.
2026
Soiree in the City
Our annual gathering brings together physicians, survivors, advocates, and changemakers on the waterfront in Lower Manhattan.
October 19, 2026 — City Vineyard, New York City
An evening of celebration, education, and purpose
Join us at City Vineyard overlooking the Hudson River for cocktails, dinner, live conversation, and a shared commitment to ending HPV cancers.
Early evening
Cocktails
Open bar, waterfront views at golden hour
Program
Voices & Science
Researchers and survivors on HPV prevention
Late evening
Auction
Live auction supporting year-round programs
